rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 26, 2024 line 1. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PREJUDGMENT RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is OFF CALENDAR, moot. Judgement was entered in this case. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YAN YUN CHEN VS. NORMA HERRERA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 26, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANTS NORMA HERRERA AND JULIAN RODRIGUEZ'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff including the following incomplete language in the operative notice in all caps, bolded and underlined: "SCHOOL CHILDREN-TENANT PROTECTIVE STATUS". Plaintiff's invitation to disregard this language and only read the language that follows this statement is not supported by any citation to authority. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 516 ELLIS I7, LP VS. NATALIA PEREZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 26, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT NATALIA PEREZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend to provide a proper verification. "When the pleading is verified by the attorney, or any other person except one of the parties, he or she shall set forth in the affidavit the reasons why it is not made by one of the parties." CCP 446. Explanation provided in opposition to the motion does not satisfy this requirement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BEHROUZ HALALI VS. RODRIGO MENDOZA LUNA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 26, 2024 line 11. DEFENDANT RODRIGO LUNA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The operative Notice is fatally defective as CCP 1161(2) requires "three days' notice, excluding Saturdays and Sundays and other judicial holidays shall have been serve upon the tenant." =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
